text:
There is a wiki on Mathematics, Physics, and Philosophy discussed from the category theory point of view.
<https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/HomePage>

They also have a list of resources: <https://ncatlab.org/nlab/show/category+theory#TextBooks>

text: I know that the smalltalk syntax fits in a postcard so that would be a good selling point to show that the learning curve is manageable

text: indeed, the syntax does fit on a postcard. And in GT, we have an interactive tool to get you through it, too: <https://twitter.com/feenkcom/status/1242760105013248000?s=20>

attachments.fallback:
<https://twitter.com/feenkcom|@feenkcom>: Explaining the syntax of a language is often done through static examples and lengthy text. The time has come for a new perspective. E.g., here we highlight the #Pharo syntax for a given method live. This tool can be applied to any method.

#MoldableDevelopment with #gtoolkit <https://pbs.twimg.com/ext_tw_video_thumb/1242759939728211968/pu/img/e5ldjt1mr3TLsxNL.jpg>
attachments.text:
Explaining the syntax of a language is often done through static examples and lengthy text. The time has come for a new perspective. E.g., here we highlight the #Pharo syntax for a given method live. This tool can be applied to any method.

#MoldableDevelopment with #gtoolkit

elements.text: indeed, the syntax does fit on a postcard. And in GT, we have an interactive tool to get you through it, too:

text: I think I’m going to learn Pharo just so I can use this. The environment is too compelling

text:
In any case, we do agree that as the environment gets more and more complete, our focus is shifting towards documentation of all sorts.

Just now, there is a course on Software Modeling and Analysis that is based on GT exercises: <http://scg.unibe.ch/teaching/sma>. It also includes a few demos.

text: I have been playing with GToolkit for a while, and it’s my main Pharo UI now. It’s definitely worth a look for anyone interested in user interfaces for computing systems. Not sure how accessible it is without some Pharo background.

text:
We will organize a set of live session in the GT Discord community. The next one is an introductory session and will be held on Tuesday, October 13, 5PM CET: <https://discord.gg/FTJr9gP>

You are all welcome to join.

Of course, if you’d want to have a dedicated channel here, we’d be happy to offer guidance here as well.
